nodes.label: HBAR
nodes.name: HBAR
nodes.detail: Exact value by definition in SI 2019 redefinition. ℏ = h/(2π) = 1.054571817...×10⁻³⁴ J·s.

meta.notes: Exact by definition in SI 2019.

nodes.label: K_B
nodes.name: K_B
nodes.detail: Exact value by definition in SI 2019 redefinition. k_B = 1.380649×10⁻²³ J/K.

meta.notes: Exact by definition in SI 2019.

nodes.label: A_BOHR
nodes.name: A_BOHR
nodes.detail: Bohr radius a₀ = 5.29177210903(80)×10⁻¹¹ m. NIST SP 961.

meta.source: NIST Atomic Weights and Isotopic Compositions

nodes.label: Rb87.a_s
nodes.name: Rb87.a_s
nodes.detail: Page 2: "aF=2 = +100.4(1)a0" for the F=2 hyperfine state. Steinhauer uses |F=2, mF=2> (confirmed in Wang 2017 Table II). 100.4 a_0 × 5.2918e-11 m/a_0 = 5.313e-9 m ≈ 5.31 nm.

meta.notes: CODE HAS WRONG VALUE: 109 a_0 = 5.77 nm. Correct: 100.4 a_0 = 5.31 nm. Impact is ~8% in a_s, ~4% in c_s. Secondary to the omega_perp 4× error but still needs fixing for publication accuracy.

meta.source: NIST Atomic Weights and Isotopic Compositions

nodes.label: K39.a_s
nodes.name: K39.a_s
nodes.detail: K-39 has a broad Feshbach resonance near 402 G that allows tuning a_s from near-zero to thousands of a_0 (D'Errico et al., New J. Phys. 9, 223 (2007)). 50 nm ≈ 945 a_0 is a design choice for a projected experiment — chosen as a moderate value that gives reasonable BEC density and sound speed.

meta.source: Design choice — K-39 Feshbach resonance allows wide tuning
meta.notes: Tunable parameter, not a fixed measurement. 50 nm = 945 a_0 is within the broad K-39 Feshbach resonance range. This is a design choice — Heidelberg could choose any value by tuning the magnetic field.

meta.source: NIST Atomic Weights and Isotopic Compositions

nodes.label: Na23.a_s
nodes.name: Na23.a_s
nodes.detail: Na-23 triplet scattering length a_T = 54.54(20) a_0 (Knoop et al. 2011, high-precision Feshbach spectroscopy). Older value: 52.98 a_0 (Tiesinga et al. 1996). 2.75 nm ≈ 52 a_0 — matches older reference. With Knoop: 54.54 a_0 = 2.887 nm.

meta.notes: Value 2.75 nm (52 a_0) is from older Tiesinga 1996. More precise Knoop 2011: 54.54 a_0 = 2.887 nm. ~5% difference. Acceptable for projected experiment.

nodes.label: Steinhauer.density_upstream
nodes.name: Steinhauer.density_upstream
nodes.detail: Page 13 text: "nmax ~ 120 μm⁻¹" (peak density from 3D GP). Fig. 2 shows position-dependent profile. Horizon region is well below peak: ~40-60 μm⁻¹. 5e7 m⁻¹ = 50 μm⁻¹ chosen as approximate horizon-region value.

meta.notes: Position-dependent quantity. Peak = 120 μm⁻¹, horizon ~ 40-60 μm⁻¹. Our value of 50 μm⁻¹ is an approximation, not an exact measurement.

nodes.label: Steinhauer.velocity_upstream
nodes.name: Steinhauer.velocity_upstream
nodes.detail: No published table with this exact value found. Appears to be derived from Mach ~ 0.74 × c_s, where c_s itself is computed from (now-known-wrong) omega_perp = 500 Hz. With corrected c_s ≈ 0.55 mm/s, Mach 0.74 gives v ≈ 0.41 mm/s, not 0.85 mm/s. VALUE NEEDS RECALCULATION after omega_perp fix.

meta.source: NEEDS IDENTIFICATION — derived from Mach number assumption
meta.notes: CIRCULAR DERIVATION: v = Mach × c_s, but c_s was computed from wrong omega_perp. With corrected params, v ≈ 0.41 mm/s. Also: Steinhauer may report flow velocity directly in density profile figures — needs check.

nodes.label: Steinhauer.omega_perp
nodes.name: Steinhauer.omega_perp
nodes.detail: Table II: "radial trap frequency ν = 123 Hz". Also confirmed in text: "ω_x/ω_ρ = 4.5 Hz/123 Hz". The paper labels this ω_ρ (radial) = ω_perp (transverse). This is for Steinhauer 2014 apparatus (same trap as 2019).

meta.notes: CRITICAL: Code uses 2π×500 Hz but source says 123 Hz. The 500 Hz value has NO published source. 123 Hz → g₁D = 8.63e-40 J·m, c_s ≈ 0.55 mm/s, κ ≈ 290 s⁻¹ (matches published T_H). 500 Hz → g₁D = 3.82e-39 J·m, c_s = 1.15 mm/s, κ = 21.9 s⁻¹ (wrong by 13×). Also: deep research arXiv ID 1706.01483 was WRONG (combustion paper). Correct arXiv is 1605.01027.

nodes.label: Heidelberg.density_upstream
nodes.name: Heidelberg.density_upstream
nodes.detail: Heidelberg (Oberthaler group) has published analog COSMOLOGY (Viermann et al., Nature 611, 260, 2022), NOT analog Hawking radiation. No transonic flow or sonic horizon experiment exists. This density is a projection for a hypothetical Hawking experiment.

meta.notes: PROJECTED: No Heidelberg Hawking experiment exists.

nodes.label: Heidelberg.velocity_upstream
nodes.name: Heidelberg.velocity_upstream
nodes.detail: Projected value for hypothetical Hawking experiment.

meta.notes: PROJECTED: No Heidelberg Hawking experiment exists.

nodes.label: Heidelberg.omega_perp
nodes.name: Heidelberg.omega_perp
nodes.detail: Projected value. No published source for this trap frequency in a Hawking radiation context.

meta.notes: PROJECTED: Same unsourced 500 Hz as Steinhauer. Heidelberg uses 2D geometry for cosmology experiments — different confinement than 1D BEC.

nodes.label: Trento.density_upstream
nodes.name: Trento.density_upstream
nodes.detail: Trento (Carusotto/Ferrari group) proposed spin-sonic Hawking in Berti et al., Comptes Rendus Physique 25 (2025). Paper uses dimensionless parameters only — no experimental density published.

meta.notes: PROJECTED: Spin-sonic horizon is a theoretical proposal, not realized.

nodes.label: Trento.velocity_upstream
nodes.name: Trento.velocity_upstream
nodes.detail: Projected value for hypothetical spin-sonic experiment.

meta.notes: PROJECTED: No Trento Hawking experiment exists.

nodes.label: Trento.omega_perp
nodes.name: Trento.omega_perp
nodes.detail: Projected value. Same unsourced 500 Hz as other platforms.

meta.notes: PROJECTED: Same unsourced 500 Hz. Trento uses two-component Na-23 with spin degrees of freedom — trap parameters would differ from single-component BEC.

nodes.label: POLARITON_MASS
nodes.name: POLARITON_MASS
nodes.detail: Page 4: "we extract the effective polariton mass at the working point, m* = 7.0 × 10⁻³⁵ kg." Fitted from measured spectrum.

nodes.label: Paris_long.c_s
nodes.name: Paris_long.c_s
nodes.detail: Code uses 1.0e6 m/s (1 µm/ps). Falque et al. (2025) page 5: "speed of sound cs(x) ≈ 0.40 µm ps⁻¹" = 4.0e5 m/s. Code value is 2.5× higher than the measured value. The 1 µm/ps value may come from Jacquet 2022 (different experiment) or be a projected estimate for a higher-density polariton fluid.

meta.notes: DISCREPANCY: Code 1.0e6 m/s vs Falque measured 4.0e5 m/s. Need to verify Jacquet 2022 source and determine if 1.0 µm/ps is from a different experiment or a projection.

nodes.label: Paris_long.xi
nodes.name: Paris_long.xi
nodes.detail: Code uses 2.0 µm. Falque et al. (2025) page 5: "healing length ξ is typically 3.4 µm upstream and 4.0 µm downstream." Code value is ~50% lower than measured.

meta.source: DISCREPANCY: Falque 2025 reports 3.4-4.0 µm, not 2.0 µm
meta.notes: DISCREPANCY: Code 2.0 µm vs Falque measured 3.4-4.0 µm.

nodes.label: Paris_long.kappa
nodes.name: Paris_long.kappa
nodes.detail: SLM-controlled horizon, projected surface gravity ~0.05 THz.

meta.notes: Falque demonstrated polariton horizons but did not report κ directly. This is an order-of-magnitude estimate.

nodes.label: Paris_long.tau_cav
nodes.name: Paris_long.tau_cav
nodes.detail: Falque 2025 reports ℏγ ≈ 80 µeV → γ = 1.2e11 s⁻¹ → τ ≈ 8 ps. 100 ps is a projected value for a next-generation long-lifetime cavity. Such cavities exist (e.g., Ballarini 2017) but Falque did not use one.

meta.source: Projected — NOT from Falque 2025 (actual cavity has τ ≈ 8 ps)
meta.notes: TIER CORRECTED: Was MEASURED, now PROJECTED. Falque actual cavity τ ≈ 8 ps.
Derived from tau_cav, not independently measured.

nodes.label: Paris_long.gamma_phonon_dim
nodes.name: Paris_long.gamma_phonon_dim
nodes.detail: Dimensionless phonon damping, subdominant to cavity decay. Order-of-magnitude estimate.

meta.notes: Estimated, not measured or published.

nodes.label: Paris_ultralong.tau_cav
nodes.name: Paris_ultralong.tau_cav
nodes.detail: Ultra-long-lifetime cavity τ = 300 ps. This is an aspirational target, not from any published experiment. Falque actual cavity τ ≈ 8 ps.

meta.notes: TIER CORRECTED: Was MEASURED, now PROJECTED.

nodes.label: Paris_standard.tau_cav
nodes.name: Paris_standard.tau_cav
nodes.detail: Standard GaAs microcavity lifetime ~3 ps. This is a typical order-of-magnitude value, not from a specific measurement.

meta.source: Typical GaAs microcavity value — no specific paper
meta.notes: Order-of-magnitude value. For publication, need either a specific source or explicit acknowledgment that this is a representative value.

nodes.label: MAJORANA_GAMMA_8x8
nodes.name: MAJORANA_GAMMA_8x8
nodes.detail: Real 8×8 representation of Cl(4,0). The 4 gamma matrices satisfy {Γ^a, Γ^b} = 2δ^{ab}, are symmetric (Γ^a)^T = Γ^a, and real. Unique up to orthogonal conjugation. Constructed via tensor products of Pauli matrices: Γ^1=σ₁⊗σ₀⊗σ₁, Γ^2=σ₁⊗σ₀⊗σ₃, Γ^3=σ₁⊗σ₁⊗σ₀, Γ^4=σ₁⊗σ₃⊗σ₀.

meta.notes: Lean: majorana_gamma_squared_identity, majorana_anticommutation (MajoranaKramers.lean). All proved, zero sorry.

nodes.label: MAJORANA_J1
nodes.name: MAJORANA_J1
nodes.detail: Charge conjugation matrix in the 8×8 Majorana basis. J₁ is antisymmetric (J₁^T = -J₁), satisfies J₁² = -I, and commutes with all Γ^a: [J₁, Γ^a] = 0. J₁Γ^a is antisymmetric, enabling nonzero Majorana bilinear Ψ^T(J₁Γ^a)Ψ. Part of the quaternionic commutant of Cl(4,0).

meta.notes: Lean: quaternionic_structure_squared, cg_antisymmetric (MajoranaKramers.lean). All proved, zero sorry.

nodes.label: MAJORANA_J2
nodes.name: MAJORANA_J2
nodes.detail: Kramers operator. Antisymmetric, J₂² = -I, {J₁, J₂} = 0, [J₂, Γ^a] = 0, [J₂, S] = 0 for S ∈ Spin(4). The key property {J₂, A} = 0 for the fermion matrix A guarantees Pf(A) has definite sign → sign-problem-free MC.

meta.notes: Lean: kramers_anticommutation, kramers_pfaffian_definite_sign, adw_sign_problem_free (MajoranaKramers.lean). All proved, zero sorry.

nodes.label: MAJORANA_J3
nodes.name: MAJORANA_J3
nodes.detail: Third quaternionic structure. J₃ = J₁J₂, satisfies J₃² = -I, {J₁, J₃} = 0, {J₂, J₃} = 0. Together J₁, J₂, J₃ generate the quaternion algebra ℍ, the commutant of Cl(4,0) in M₈(ℝ).

meta.notes: Lean: j1_j2_anticommute (MajoranaKramers.lean). Proved, zero sorry.
